articles.title: US stocks today: S&P 500, Nasdaq close lower amid mixed show by tech stocks ahead of earnings
articles.description: U.S. markets ended mixed with Nasdaq under pressure as software stocks dragged while chip stocks gained. Investors remained cautious ahead of key earnings from Alphabet and Tesla Inc.. Rising oil prices and geopolitical tensions added inflation concerns, keeping market sentiment volatile despite ongoing AI-driven optimism.

articles.title: Tech Stocks Waver as Investors Eye AI Earnings Amid Geopolitical Tensions
articles.description: The S&P 500 and Nasdaq declined as investors anticipated earnings reports from key tech companies like Alphabet and Tesla to assess AI investments. Trading was mixed, with semiconductor stocks outperforming software stocks. Geopolitical tensions and rising crude oil prices added caution. Federal Reserve rate decisions remain a focus.

articles.title: Va. consumers face return of $4 gas as Iran war intensifies
articles.description: Gasoline prices rose above $4 a gallon in parts of Virginia on Wednesday, as the United States and Iran traded military blows over control of the Strait of Hormuz, last week ending a ceasefire that had brought consumers temporary relief at the pump.

articles.title: Tensions Flare: Iran-U.S. Maritime Conflict Heightens Oil Market Risks
articles.description: U.S. President Donald Trump intensifies tensions with Iran, threatening infrastructure strikes following Iranian Houthi allied threats on key maritime routes impacting oil exports. Amid growing regional conflict, global oil prices surged, prompting diplomatic maneuvers to negotiate a truce. Military engagements continue, escalating geopolitical instability and economic repercussions.

articles.title: Gas prices averaged $3.80 in Iowa the week of July 22
articles.description: The price of regular unleaded gasoline rose 12 cents from last week's price and is currently averaging $3.80 across Iowa, according to AAA and the Iowa Department of Agriculture and Land Stewardship's weekly fuel report.

articles.title: Markets Stir Amid U.S.-Iran Tensions and Japanese Yen Recovery
articles.description: Economic tensions escalate as the dollar fluctuates following the U.S.-Iran conflict, affecting oil prices and inflation fears. The yen shows signs of recovery as Japan deliberates on potential intervention, balancing currency stabilization with inflation risks. Global financial markets remain volatile amid geopolitical uncertainties.

articles.title: Escalating Global Supply Risks Underpin Crude Oil Prices
articles.description: September WTI crude oil (CLU26 ) on Wednesday closed up +2.49 (+2.95%), and September RBOB gasoline (RBU26 ) closed up +0.0186 (+0.58%). Crude oil and gasoline prices settled sharply higher on Wednesday, with crude posting a 6-week high and gasoline...

articles.title: S&P 500 Opens Lower, Falls 0.30% to 7,486.83 Amid Oil-Driven Inflation Fears
articles.description: The S&P 500 opened lower on Wednesday, falling 22.37 points or 0.30% to 7,486.83, following Tuesday's 0.89% close at 7,509.20. Rising oil prices with Brent crude above $92, driven by the 11th consecutive night of U.S. strikes on Iran, reignited inflation concerns. Despite nearly 88% of reporting S&P 500 companies beating profit estimates per FactSet, CME FedWatch data shows a 69% probability of at least a quarter-point rate hike by September, keeping investor sentiment cautious.

articles.title: Hot US Weather Forecasts Boost Nat-Gas Prices
articles.description: August Nymex natural gas (NGQ26 ) on Wednesday closed up +0.060 (+2.09%). Nat-gas prices settled sharply higher on Wednesday amid forecasts for hotter US weather, potentially boosting nat-gas demand from electricity providers to power increased air-c...

articles.title: Oil, LNG and AI drive new energy opportunities
articles.description: Oil prices, LNG disruptions and AI-driven power demand are reshaping opportunities across the energy sector.
